---
title: "Roadmap"
---

How every module depends on the others. Blue nodes are reusable
[foundations](../../foundations/README.md) modules shared across courses; the green node
is a prerequisite *course*; arrows mean "needed for".

Like every course, this one assumes the notation grammar in
[reading-math-notation](../../foundations/reading-math-notation/lesson.qmd) — take it
first if the symbols aren't yet second nature.

This course also **builds on** [Logistic Regression](../logistic-regression/syllabus.md)
(a GWAS effect estimate $\hat\beta$ is a log odds ratio, exactly the log-odds-scale
coefficient you fitted there) and reuses its anchors rather than re-teaching them; if
rusty, skim its syllabus first.

```{mermaid}
flowchart TD
    classDef foundation fill:#e8f0fe,stroke:#4285f4
    classDef priorcourse fill:#e6f4ea,stroke:#34a853

    F1["probability-and-odds<br/>(p, odds = p/(1−p))"]:::foundation
    F2["distributions-and-densities<br/>(dnorm, N(mu, sigma^2))"]:::foundation
    F3["likelihood-and-log-likelihood<br/>(likelihood as p varies)"]:::foundation
    F4["priors-posteriors-and-updating<br/>(prior x likelihood)"]:::foundation
    F5["hypothesis-tests-p-values-and-confidence-intervals<br/>(Z = est/SE, the p-value)"]:::foundation
    F6["marginal-likelihood-and-model-evidence<br/>(average over the prior; N(0, V+W))"]:::foundation
    F7["bayes-factors-and-posterior-odds<br/>(posterior odds = prior odds x BF)"]:::foundation

    C1["course: logistic-regression<br/>(effect = log odds ratio)"]:::priorcourse

    M1["01 · The summary of an association<br/>(beta-hat, V, Z, the p-value)"]
    M2["02 · A prior on the effect<br/>(N(0,W); marginals N(0,V) vs N(0,V+W))"]
    M3["03 · The Approximate Bayes Factor<br/>(lABF = 0.5(log(1−r) + r Z^2))"]
    M4["04 · Posterior probability of association<br/>(PPA; the p-value comparison)"]
    M5["05 · Capstone<br/>(decode Wakefield & coloc)"]

    F3 --> F6
    F4 --> F6
    F2 --> F6
    F6 --> F7
    F1 --> F7
    F4 --> F7

    C1 --> M1
    F5 --> M1
    M1 --> M2
    F2 --> M2
    F6 --> M2
    M2 --> M3
    F3 --> M3
    M3 --> M4
    F7 --> M4
    F1 --> M4
    F5 --> M4
    M4 --> M5
```

## Suggested order

1. Foundation: [probability-and-odds](../../foundations/probability-and-odds/lesson.qmd) —
   new? take it fully; done before? skim its pronunciation table.
2. Foundation: [distributions-and-densities](../../foundations/distributions-and-densities/lesson.qmd) —
   same rule.
3. Foundation: [likelihood-and-log-likelihood](../../foundations/likelihood-and-log-likelihood/lesson.qmd) —
   same rule.
4. Foundation: [priors-posteriors-and-updating](../../foundations/priors-posteriors-and-updating/lesson.qmd) —
   same rule.
5. Foundation: [hypothesis-tests-p-values-and-confidence-intervals](../../foundations/hypothesis-tests-p-values-and-confidence-intervals/lesson.qmd) —
   same rule.
6. Foundation: [marginal-likelihood-and-model-evidence](../../foundations/marginal-likelihood-and-model-evidence/lesson.qmd) —
   new for this course; the engine of the ABF.
7. Foundation: [bayes-factors-and-posterior-odds](../../foundations/bayes-factors-and-posterior-odds/lesson.qmd) —
   new for this course; take it after marginal-likelihood.
8. [01 · The summary of an association](modules/01-the-summary-of-an-association/lesson.qmd)
   (+ [practice](modules/01-the-summary-of-an-association/practice.qmd))
9. [02 · A prior on the effect](modules/02-a-prior-on-the-effect/lesson.qmd)
   (+ [practice](modules/02-a-prior-on-the-effect/practice.qmd))
10. [03 · The Approximate Bayes Factor](modules/03-the-approximate-bayes-factor/lesson.qmd)
    (+ [practice](modules/03-the-approximate-bayes-factor/practice.qmd))
11. [04 · Posterior probability of association](modules/04-posterior-probability-of-association/lesson.qmd)
    (+ [practice](modules/04-posterior-probability-of-association/practice.qmd))
12. [05 · Capstone — reading Wakefield's ABF](modules/05-capstone-reading-wakefields-abf/lesson.qmd)
    (+ [practice](modules/05-capstone-reading-wakefields-abf/practice.qmd))

After each lesson, do its practice page before moving on — the problems are the part that
makes it stick. External videos/posts for each module: [resources](resources.md).
